title: screenplay_pack
description: Intermediate JSON produced by english-screenplay-writer. All fields feed into storyboard-builder and the final .docx export.

episode_briefs.description: One entry per episode. Mirrors the [EPISODE BRIEF] block at the top of each episode in the .docx.

plot_summary.description: 2-4 sentences summarising all scenes in the episode.

opening_hook.description: What grabs the audience in the first few seconds.

cold_open_focus.description: The specific visible element required in the first 3-5 seconds of the episode.

closing_hook.description: The unresolved suspense or crisis at episode end. Must match the final scene's [SCENE ENDING].

core_satisfaction_beat.description: Satisfaction type + landing point. Must match outline episode_outlines[n].satisfaction_beat.

target_emotion.description: Dominant viewer emotion at the final second of the episode.

episodes.description: Full screenplay text, one entry per episode.

screenplay_markdown.description: Full Markdown of the episode. Must use the 4-part scene structure per rules/12_剧本规范.md.

scene_crosswalk.description: Index of every scene across all episodes. Used by storyboard-builder to map scenes to shot rows.

characters.description: Comma-separated display names of all characters present.

core_event.description: One sentence — the primary narrative event of the scene.

scene_result.description: One sentence — how the situation has changed by scene end.

outline_ref.description: Which outline field this scene primarily delivers. Each episode's scenes must collectively include core_event and turn.

key_props.description: Optional — prop IDs from setting_bible that appear in this scene.

scene_ending_type.description: The [SCENE ENDING] type used in this scene.

action_block_count.description: Number of action blocks in [MAIN BODY]. Dialogue scenes ≥5; conflict scenes ≥7; emotional/reveal scenes ≥6; quick transition ≥3.
